mode_descriptions.bounded_internal_audit: Use synthesis as the starting compression, reread only the most decisive upstream artifacts, and avoid broad re-research by default.
mode_descriptions.targeted_escalation: Use a bounded internal audit plus a small number of targeted checks when a narrow ambiguity or meaningful edge justifies it.
mode_descriptions.not_ready_reopen_recommended: If responsible verification would require a much broader reread or new research pass, prefer a not-ready/non-action packet over pretending bounded verification was enough.

verification_policy.hard_rule: Decision-Maker is allowed to do targeted additional verification, but should not become a routine second synthesis pass. If adequate verification would require broader reread or new research, prefer a not-ready or non-action packet.

source_excerpt.decision_handoff_body:
# Decision summary

Bitcoin is very likely to be above $68,000 on the April 12 governing settlement print, but the best synthesis view remains slightly below the market because this contract resolves on one future Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute close at 12:00 ET rather than on broad spot conditions. Post-synthesis, I estimate roughly 0.92 to 0.94 for Yes, with the market’s 0.9595 implying a modest overconfidence premium rather than a large exploitable mispricing.

## Why this may matter now

Market implied 0.9595 Yes. My syndicated range is 0.92 to 0.94 Yes. That leaves at most a marginal lean against the market rather than a strong actionable edge. The likely mispricing, if any, is that the market treats current distance above strike almost like resolution certainty even though the contract depends on one future Binance minute close.

## Shift versus swarm baseline

There is no material difference from the swarm-implied center of roughly 0.93. Independent checking supported the same basic interpretation: the swarm was directionally right and not obviously missing a major catalyst or contract trap. The synthesis therefore largely endorses the swarm center rather than meaningfully revising it.

## Edge verification status

Independent verification quality is medium. I independently checked fresh Binance ticker data and recent 1-minute klines, both of which showed BTCUSDT still materially above 68000. I also cross-checked the contract mechanics from the raw lane work: Binance BTC/USDT, 1-minute candle, 12:00 ET, final close, with noon ET mapping to 16:00 UTC. This is enough to verify that the swarm’s negative edge versus market is plausible and not invented. Verification is not high because the central risk cannot be eliminated before the future settlement minute occurs, and the Polymarket page URL fetch itself was not cleanly retrievable in this pass.

## Compression toward market

No meaningful compression toward market was needed. The independent checks did not reveal stronger support for the market’s near-certainty than the swarm had already considered, but they also did not uncover a hidden flaw severe enough to widen the anti-market gap. So the synthesis stays near the swarm center rather than moving materially toward 0.9595.

## Timing and catalyst posture

The only catalyst that really matters is the settlement window itself: the April 12 12:00 ET / 16:00 UTC Binance BTC/USDT minute close. Edge decay is likely if BTC simply remains comfortably above strike into late morning ET, because the market and synthesis will converge toward realized resolution. Waiting can improve accuracy, but it also reduces any small remaining edge because uncertainty burns off quickly in these narrow timing markets.

## Key blockers

No major blocker prevents taking a view, but there are three caution flags: the decisive settlement candle was still in the future, crypto can move several percent in the remaining window, and minute-candle implementation details retain minor residual ambiguity. Those are operator-caution blockers more than research blockers.

## Best countercase

Best countercase: No can still happen without any broad bearish regime shift if BTC suffers a roughly 5% downside move before noon ET or if a Binance-specific dislocation hits the exact settlement minute. Base-rate and risk-manager represented this countercase most clearly.

## What would change the view

A fresh check showing BTCUSDT compressing toward 69k or below before late morning ET would push the estimate materially lower. Verified evidence of a different settlement-minute interpretation would also change the view. Conversely, stable trading well above 71k into the last hour before settlement would move the estimate closer to the market.

## Recommended next action

Wait for the final pre-settlement Binance check, then hand to the decision-maker if action is still relevant. No swarm rerun is needed unless price action changes sharply or a rule-interpretation issue emerges.

## Verification impact

Yes, the synthesis added a bounded truth-finding pass. Fresh Binance ticker and recent klines confirmed that the observed cushion above 68000 still existed. Cross-lane comparison also showed the sidecars were broadly faithful to the raw findings and that no persona had a materially different factual picture. This extra verification did not change the direction or center much; it mainly increased confidence that the swarm’s modest-below-market view was coherent.
